evals.name: define-from-active-spec
evals.prompt: Let's start implementing the collaborative itineraries spec. Let's begin with the collaborator data model and the django-guardian integration.
expected_output: .ai/session/plan.md created from plan-template.md
.ai/session/tasks.md created with phases declaring depends-on and reversibility
.ai/session/walkthrough.md created as a stub
Tasks are TDD-ordered (failing test before implementation within each phase)
Plan cites both the spec and the reference-index entry for django-guardian
Agent paused for approval before any execution; no source files modified
If any ambiguity existed (e.g. role storage), it was resolved via a questionnaire — no [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] block in the artifact

evals.name: define-independent-plan
evals.prompt: We are seeing N+1 problems in the itinerary listing. We need to optimize the queries in the list view.
expected_output: plan.md created with spec field empty in frontmatter
Technical approach section explains the use of select_related / prefetch_related
tasks.md has at least one phase covering profiling (confirm the N+1) and one for the fix
TDD posture: a perf or query-count assertion exists as a test task before the optimization task
Sources consulted includes the constitution and (if relevant) the reference-index for django-debug-toolbar
Agent paused for approval

evals.name: execute-with-mid-phase-discrepancy

context.simulated_obstacle: Next task references an Itinerary.owner field; the model actually uses Itinerary.created_by

expected_output: Agent verified git status before doing anything
Agent surfaced the field name discrepancy (owner vs created_by) and stopped to ask, instead of guessing
If the developer approves the rename in tasks.md, the agent also amended plan.md (Plan amendments section) and noted the deviation in walkthrough.md
No further phase was started in the same turn; the agent paused at the phase boundary
A chore(wip) commit message was suggested but not executed

evals.name: summarize-with-adr-proposal

session_files.walkthrough.md: 3 phase blocks, one with a structural decision: roles as CharField choices

expected_output: .ai/session/<slug>-summary.md created using plan-summary-template.md
Summary is short (target one screen, no walkthrough-style narration)
Spec coverage section marks the user stories closed by this plan
An ADR proposal is included (CharField vs roles table), status: Proposed, with context/decision/alternatives/consequences
A conventional-commit message is suggested (feat:...) and explicitly noted as a squash target option
Agent did NOT run cleanup; explicitly handed off the next step to the developer
Agent did NOT write directly to .ai/memory/adr-log.md
